schemaVersion.description: Config format version. Always 2 for this file. A legacy schemaVersion 1 .claude/project.json (old delegation.devModel/reviewModel/prReviewModel keys) is still read and normalized on load, with a deprecation warning; init-config.sh converts it to this v2 project.yaml shape.

project.description: Identity of the repo this config governs: its name, main branch, and branch-naming convention.

name.description: Project name. Used to qualify feedback-feed refs across a multi-project archive (<project.name>#N) and shown in status/summary output.

description.description: Free-text one-line summary of the project, shown in setup/status output. Optional.

mainBranch.description: The branch tasks merge into (e.g. main, master). Used by gate.sh, board.sh, and the merge/sync protocols as the diff/merge base.

branchPattern.description: Branch name template. Placeholders: <prefix> (spec taskPrefix, lowercased), <id> (task number, zero-padded as issued), <slug> (kebab-case title).

boards.description: GitHub Projects this config drives (usually one; a monorepo may declare several). specs[].board and delegation route to a board by its `id`. The first entry is the default board.

id.description: Local identifier; specs reference boards by this id. First board is the default.

provider.description: Board backend. Only 'github-project' (a GitHub Projects v2 board, driven via `gh project`/`gh api` through board.sh) is supported today.

repo.description: owner/name of the repo where issues live.

projectNumber.description: The Project's number as shown in its GitHub URL (…/projects/<N>).

projectId.description: GraphQL node id (PVT_...). Discover with: board.sh fields

fields.description: GraphQL field/option ids for this board's single-select fields, so board.sh can mutate items without re-discovering ids on every call. Discover with `board.sh fields`.

priority.description: Option key order in `options` = priority order, highest first.

estimate.description: Optional numeric estimate field (e.g. story points/hours), used by telemetry.py's estimate-vs-actual report.

fieldId.description: GraphQL node id of the estimate number field.

statusFlow.description: Ordered pipeline, e.g. [Backlog, In progress, In review, QA, Ready, Deployed]. Names must match status field option keys.

labels.description: Repo labels applied by `board.sh add`/`bug`. `board.sh ensure-labels` idempotently creates any of these missing on the repo (also run by setup-project / seed-board.sh).

bug.description: Label applied by `board.sh bug` (alias of `add --type bug`). Default: type:bug.

feature.description: Label applied by `board.sh add --type feature`. Default: type:feature.

inbound.description: Label applied by `board.sh add --type inbound` (ad-hoc captures via create-inbound). Default: inbound.

specs.description: The spec document(s) this project's board tasks are drawn from. Usually one; a monorepo may run several specs against the same or different boards.

title.description: Human-readable spec title, shown in status/summary output.

board.description: boards[].id this spec's tasks live on.

specPath.description: Repo-relative path to the spec document.

backlogPath.description: Repo-relative path to the backlog doc with per-task acceptance criteria.

taskPrefix.description: Task id prefix; issue titles look like '<taskPrefix>-<number>: <title>'. Must be unique across specs.

epics.description: Ordered by build sequence. Position in this array is the sequencing rank.

id.description: Epic identifier, referenced by other epics' blockedBy[].epic.

taskRanges.description: Inclusive [lo, hi] task-number ranges belonging to this epic.

blockedBy.description: Epic-level dependency guards: this epic's tasks are not picked by next.py until every named epic reaches its untilStatus.

epic.description: Epic id (same spec) that must complete first.

untilStatus.description: Every task of that epic must have reached this status (or later in statusFlow).

invariants.description: Hard rules agents must never violate (fed into implementation briefs verbatim).

work.description: PR-less local delivery + board-sync batching policy (SW-079). Absent == { type: pr } (today's default: push a branch, open a GitHub PR, review + merge via gh).

type.description: How a task's work is delivered. pr (default): push the branch, open a GitHub PR, review it, `gh pr merge`. local: the branch stays local — review is `git diff <main>..<branch>`, approval is recorded as an ISSUE comment, and the orchestrator squash-merges locally (role attribution in the commit) and pushes main (skipped, with a stated reason, if the repo has no remote).
enumDescriptions: Push the branch, open a GitHub PR, review + merge it via gh (today's default).
No PR is ever opened — review the local diff, record approval as an issue comment, and squash-merge locally.

sync.description: Only valid when type: local — governs WHEN queued board mutations flush. Rejected (validate-config.py) when type is pr, since PR delivery already syncs the board in real time as a side effect of each step.

mode.description: Board-sync cadence for local delivery. In every mode, a `blocked` or `new-item` event ALWAYS syncs immediately (the safety valve) — never held back for batching. See scripts/work-mode.sh should-sync.
enumDescriptions: Every board transition syncs immediately, same as PR delivery.
Board mutations batch via the board queue and flush when a task closes.
Board mutations batch and flush at iteration/session boundaries.
Board mutations only flush via an explicit board.sh flush call.

docs.description: Documentation sets the reviewer enforces: when a diff touches files under a set's `covers` globs and changes behavior/contracts/usage, that set's docs must be updated in the same PR (or the PR must state why not). Standalone repo: one entry covering everything. Monorepo: one entry per package/app plus an optional root entry.

path.description: Repo-relative docs location — a directory (docs/) or a file (README.md).

covers.description: Code-path globs this doc set documents. A diff touching a covered path puts this set in review scope.

notes.description: What belongs in this doc set (helps the reviewer judge 'needed a doc update' vs not).

commands.description: Project-specific shell commands the workflow's scripts shell out to (gate.sh, dev-up skill, etc).

gate.description: Single command that must be green before a task advances to review.

gateFast.description: Optional fast inner-loop test command template.

devUp.description: Command that brings up the full local dev stack.

test.description: Optional standalone test command, when it differs from commands.gate (e.g. gate also lints/typechecks). Not read by any script today; documents intent for humans and future tooling.

delegation.description: Who codes/reviews/orchestrates, as whom, and on which ALLOWED models. In schemaVersion 2 models live INSIDE each identity (there is no delegation.devModel/reviewModel/prReviewModel — those legacy v1 keys are mapped into identities.<role>.models on load).

reviewerTokenEnv.description: Name of an env var holding a GitHub token for a SECOND account (machine user / bot). When set, the auto-merge approval is submitted with that token (GH_TOKEN=$<var> gh pr review --approve), so the PR shows a real distinct approver — GitHub rejects self-approval from the account that opened the PR. When absent, the verdict is posted as a review comment instead.

identities.description: Per-role agent identities (git author + allowed models), ON by default. A role maps to ONE identity or an ARRAY of identities (monorepo routing via `covers`). name/email are templates resolved per-clone by scripts/identity.sh: {name} -> git config user.name, {local}/{domain} -> git config user.email parts — so each person's clone attributes agent commits to their own plus-addressed email. Placeholder-free values are literal (shared bot accounts). Built-in defaults: dev/reviewer/orchestrator names 'Dev Agent - {name}' / 'Reviewer Agent - {name}' / 'Orchestrator Agent - {name}', email {local}+<role>_agent@{domain}; model defaults dev ['claude-sonnet-5'], reviewer ['claude-sonnet-5','claude-sonnet-5[1m]'], orchestrator none. Set a role to null to make it commit as the human; set identities to false to disable all roles.

oneOf.description: Per-role identity overrides. Any key beyond dev/reviewer/orchestrator (additionalProperties) is a custom role name, resolved the same way (identity.sh <role>).

paths.description: Repo-relative locations the workflow's skills/scripts read or write, all optional with sane defaults.

handoffDir.description: Where the handoff skill writes <YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM>.md session handoffs. Default: docs/handoffs.

checkpointFile.description: Flag file the build loop checks at the start of every iteration; if present, the loop writes a handoff and stops instead of starting new work. Default: .claude/CHECKPOINT.

designDir.description: Per-epic design docs (<spec-id>-<epic-id>.md) that dev briefs ground on.

specDeltaDir.description: Per-task spec-delta files, folded into the canonical spec when the task's PR merges; applied/ holds history.

devDoc.description: Repo-relative doc with project-specific dev-stack details (ports, profiles, isolation).

methodology.description: Process knobs governing how the build loop works: TDD strictness, concurrency, graduation, UI delegation, and merge/feedback policy.

tdd.description: When true (default), every task is implemented red-first: a failing test commit precedes the implementation commit. Enforced by process/review, not a script gate.

isolationSuite.description: Test path glob for the merge-gating isolation suite, if any (e.g. tests/isolation/**).

maxInProgress.description: Maximum number of tasks worked CONCURRENTLY — THE concurrency knob. It is both the board WIP limit (enforced by next.py's picker) AND the number of parallel implementation lanes. 1 (default) = strictly sequential, one task at a time (the current behavior). N>1 = up to N tasks in parallel, each in its own git worktree + branch + dev agent — only safe for NON-overlapping tasks (see skills/build-next/references/concurrency.md). Set via the concurrency skill / concurrency.sh.

graduationThreshold.description: Note strength (SPEC §8.3) at/above which brain.py graduate-check surfaces a note as a graduation candidate (proposed destination: ROLE.md rule / specs[].invariants entry / test-or-lint). graduate-check is read-only — it proposes; graduation itself stays a human-visible retro action (brain.py graduate).

iterativeUI.description: When true (default), UI-affecting decisions are delegated to the human via the ui-options skill; a local .claude/ITERATIVE_UI_OFF flag also disables it per-clone.

autoMerge.description: When true, build-next skips human PR approval: an autonomous reviewer agent (a suitable model from delegation.identities.reviewer.models) reviews the PR, dialogues fixes with the orchestrator until it approves, then the orchestrator merges and announces the merge. Protocol: build-next skill, references/auto-review.md.

mergeMethod.description: gh pr merge strategy used by auto-merge — a per-repo decision made at setup. squash (default): linear history; per-role attribution carried as Co-authored-by trailers in the squash body + the PR's own commits. merge: preserves individual role-attributed commits on main. rebase: replays them.
enumDescriptions: Linear history; per-role attribution carried as Co-authored-by trailers in the squash commit body plus the PR link (default).
Keeps the individual role-attributed commits on main as-is.
Replays the branch's commits onto main, preserving them without a merge commit.

feedback.description: Structured per-iteration process feedback about the WORKFLOW (not the project being built), routed at retro time into backlog/brain-note/graduate/upstream/ignore. `true` is shorthand for the defaults shown below. Absent = disabled. Protocol: feedback skill; mechanics: scripts/feedback.py.

enabled.description: Whether feedback collection is on. Redundant with the `true` shorthand — set false here (inside the expanded form) to disable while keeping feed/roles/autoTriage overrides on file for later.

feed.description: Repo-relative path to the yaml feed file (sequence of --- separated loop-feedback records). Default: .claude/feedbacks/feed.yaml — a tracked, orchestrator-mediated archive (committed alongside code, never read/written by dev/reviewer subagents). The legacy, gitignored .claude/feedback/feed.yaml (singular) path is detected and blocks with a migration message when this default is in effect; an explicit override here is trusted as-is.

roles.description: Which identities are expected to emit feedback.

autoTriage.description: When true, triage routes items (e.g. backlog) without a human check-in — mirrors autoMerge's explicit-consent pattern.

roleIdentity.description: How ONE role (dev/reviewer/orchestrator/custom) commits: null (opt out — commits as the human), a single identity, or an array of identities routed by `covers` globs for monorepo per-package agents.

oneOf.description: Opt this role out — it commits as the human.

Several identities for one role (e.g. per-package dev agents). Selection: `covers` globs are matched against the task's changed/expected paths (deterministic, resolved by identity.sh), then the orchestrator judges among ties/no-match. Fallback = the first entry without `covers`, else the first entry.

identity.description: One git-author identity for a role: name/email templates plus its allowed models and (optionally) which paths route to it.

name.description: Template or literal; {name} = git config user.name. Omit to keep the role's default.

email.description: Template or literal; {local}/{domain} = git config user.email parts. Omit to keep the role's default.

models.description: The ONLY models this identity may run on — FULL nomenclature ids only (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-5[1m]', 'claude-sonnet-5', 'claude-haiku-4-5'), never shorthand like 'sonnet'. The orchestrator picks the most SUITABLE allowed model per task (cheaper/smaller for simple changes, larger-context [1m] for big diffs) — never 'always the most powerful'. The reviewer identity's models serve BOTH the two-pass implement-task review AND the auto-merge PR gate. Omit to use the role's default set.

covers.description: Optional path globs (fnmatch, ** crosses directories). When a task's changed/expected paths match, this identity is selected (monorepo per-package routing). An entry with no `covers` is the fallback.

color.description: Optional hex color for this role's brain in the neural view — nodes, synapses, cluster labels and gauges all derive from its hue. Omit to use the viewer's default palette (dev #38e0fa, reviewer #ff5b7f, orchestrator #38fad4).

singleSelectField.description: A GitHub Projects v2 single-select field: its GraphQL field id plus its option name -> option id map. Used for boards[].fields.status/priority.

fieldId.description: GraphQL node id of the single-select field (PVTSSF_...). Discover with: board.sh fields

options.description: Option name -> option id. Key order is meaningful (statusFlow order / priority order).
